turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've had this persistent cough for five days now and it's really been getting to me. It started dry, but now it's productive with yellowish-green sputum, which is really annoying.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The cough is the worst part – it's constant and makes me feel like I'm going to choke sometimes. It's been getting worse every day, and even when I take that OTC cough medicine, dextromethorphan, it only gives me some temporary relief.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started five days ago.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens all day, every hour or so. Sometimes I cough more frequently when I'm at work teaching kids, but even when I'm just sitting around relaxing at home, it still keeps coming back.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's definitely an 8 or 9 out of 10. The only time it seems to let up is when I'm sleeping, but as soon as I wake up and start moving around, it comes back with a vengeance.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: To be honest, yeah. Sometimes I feel like I'm getting winded easily or that my chest feels tight, but it's not always there. And sometimes, especially at night, I'll wake up with a bit of wheezing, which is really scary for me. But the cough is still the main thing – that and feeling really tired all the time because I've been missing work.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It definitely gets worse when I'm teaching, especially when I have to run around the classroom or help kids with activities. But even just sitting at my desk and talking can trigger a coughing fit – it's like my body is just constantly on high alert for some reason.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: I actually do wake up with night sweats almost every night. I've been soaking through my pajamas for 4 nights now, which is really concerning to me because I don't usually sweat that much at night.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: Actually, yes. Lately, when I cough, I've been experiencing some sharp pain on my right side of my chest. It's worse with deep breathing and coughing, which is really uncomfortable.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't think so. I haven't noticed anything like that, but to be honest, my main concern right now is getting rid of this cough and feeling better overall – everything else feels secondary to that.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I'm taking cetirizine 10mg as needed for my seasonal allergies. And, like I mentioned earlier, I've also been trying OTC dextromethorphan for the cough, but it hasn't really provided much relief.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about that. My last physical exam was about a year ago and everything seemed normal, but I don't recall anything being mentioned about high blood pressure or diabetes. To be honest, my main concern right now is this cough and trying to figure out what's causing it – all the other stuff feels like secondary worries for me.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure about that. My last physical exam was about a year ago, and I don't recall anything being mentioned about cholesterol levels. To be honest, my main concern right now is this persistent cough and trying to figure out what's causing it – all the other stuff feels like secondary worries for me.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I don't smoke, but I do have a social glass of wine on weekends with my partner.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: I've been trying to take care of myself, but to be honest, with this cough and missing work, I haven't had much energy for cooking or exercising. I usually try to eat a balanced diet, but lately, it's just been whatever I can grab quickly at home or when I'm out running errands. As for exercise, I used to love going for walks in the morning, but with this cough, even that feels like too much effort right now.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, and she's actually been dealing with it for a while. I'm not sure if that has any relevance to what I'm experiencing now, but my grandma did pass away from heart disease when I was younger.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My mom has asthma, and she's actually been dealing with it for a while. That might be relevant since some of my symptoms seem similar to what she experiences with her asthma. But I'm not sure if that means anything or if it's just a coincidence.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: I think it was about a year ago, during my last physical exam. Everything seemed normal back then. I don't recall anything being mentioned about any underlying conditions or issues that could be contributing to this cough now.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: Yes, I am allergic to penicillin - it causes a rash on my skin whenever I take it.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been feeling really overwhelmed with this cough and trying to figure out what's causing it. I'm worried that it might be something more serious than just a cold or allergies, especially since my mom has asthma and some of my symptoms seem similar. And on top of all that, I've been missing work and feeling like I'm not taking care of myself the way I should be.
